codeReview.summary: Verification-only feature — no code changes. The worker ran all three required verification steps: (1) full test suite (631 tests, 0 failures), (2) zero #[allow(...)] attributes across 33 kernel .rs files, (3) release build succeeds. All three claims independently verified by reviewer. The commit ID (3c28c54) correctly points to HEAD, which was the previous feature's commit (adversarial-stress-tests) — appropriate since this feature makes no code changes. The 631 test count represents 283 new tests over the 348 baseline, added across milestones 1-7 of the audit mission. The 31 dead_code warnings in release build are pre-existing and documented as out-of-scope in AGENTS.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-auditor skill is designed for TDD audit workflows (Steps 2-6 cover reading files, applying checklists, writing failing tests, fixing, verifying). For verification-only features like full-regression-run that make no code changes, this workflow is unnecessarily heavyweight. Worker correctly noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges — a 'verification-only' fast path skipping Steps 2-6 would reduce overhead for future regression-check features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker skillFeedback: 'The kernel-auditor skill is oriented toward file audits with TDD. For verification-only features like full-regression-run, a shorter verification-only path skipping Steps 2-6 would be helpful.' Worker followed procedure despite mismatch, running all verification steps correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff correctly explains that grep exit code 1 means 'no matches found' (not an error). This is a subtle correctness point — future workers running the same verification might misinterpret exit code 1 as a failure. The validation-contract or AGENTS.md could note that grep returns 1 when no matches are found, which is the expected success condition for the #[allow] check.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification: 'grep -rc #[allow system/kernel/*.rs, exitCode: 1, observation: Exit code 1 is expected from grep when no matches found — confirms zero #[allow(...)] attributes.'

summary: PASS. Verification-only feature executed correctly. All three required checks independently confirmed: 631 tests pass (283 above 348 baseline), zero #[allow(...)] attributes in kernel, release build succeeds. No code changes were made, consistent with feature description. Worker followed procedure methodically and documented results thoroughly.
